description_long: This is a classic, cylindrical fire hydrant with a domed top and a weathered, yellowish-brown paint finish, showing signs of rust and wear. The hydrant features three prominent hose connection outlets: two on the sides and one facing forward, each capped and secured with a metal chain. The body is robust and made primarily of cast metal, with visible bolts and seams along the top and base. A hexagonal operating nut is present on the top dome, used for opening the hydrant. The base is wide and circular, providing stability, and the chains are attached to the side caps to prevent loss. There are faint markings or engravings near the base, possibly indicating the manufacturer or model.
description: A weathered yellow-brown fire hydrant with three hose outlets, metal chains, and a domed top with an operating nut. The surface shows rust, wear, and faint manufacturer markings.
description_view_0: The hydrant is seen from a slight angle, highlighting the front outlet, one side outlet, and the attached chain.
description_view_1: A direct front view showing both side outlets, the front outlet, and the full length of the chains.
description_view_2: The opposite side view, focusing on the side outlet and chain, with the front outlet partially visible.
description_view_3: A rear view displaying both side outlets symmetrically and the back of the hydrant, with faint base markings visible.
